channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Some charges dropped in Frank Stronach sexual assault trial amid credibility concerns
item.description: The trial of Canadian billionaire Frank Stronach, founder of Magna International, has seen a reduction in charges from 12 to seven, following the withdrawal of five counts related to three of the orig...

item.title: How young Kenyans are mobilising voter registration to reduce political apathy
item.description: This opinion piece reflects on how young Kenyans are slowly changing the story around political apathy by actively engaging in voter registration drives across the country. It explains that, for years...

item.title: Understanding Laetare Sunday and Its Role in Lent
item.description: The Catholic Church is currently observing Lent, a sacred period where devotees renew their faith through prayer, fasting, and charitable acts, preparing for the resurrection of Jesus Christ. On the f...

item.title: Israel Reports Critical Shortage of Ballistic Missile Interceptors Amid Tensions with Iran
item.description: Israel has informed the United States that it is experiencing a critical shortage of ballistic missile interceptors, according to a recent report by Semafor, citing U.S. officials familiar with the ma...

item.title: Kimi Antonelli secures first F1 victory as Lewis Hamilton finishes third in Shanghai
item.description: Kimi Antonelli, at just 19 years old, made history at the Chinese Grand Prix by becoming the youngest driver ever to claim pole position in Formula 1. The Italian achieved this after George Russell en...

item.title: Leaders encouraged to use strategic training to boost public-private cooperation
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Zanzibar Minister of Finance and Planning, Juma Akili, has called upon leaders from different sectors to actively use the skills and networks they gained from a one-week strategic lead...

item.title: Governor Nehal Hashmi Promises Youth Employment and Education Reforms in Sindh
item.description: Sindh's newly sworn-in Governor Nehal Hashmi, addressing his first press conference in Karachi, emphasised that his role is a duty rather than a privilege. He promised to expand the Prime Minister’s Y...

item.title: Philippine Women’s Bobsled Team Competes in North American Cup for Experience and Growth
item.description: The Philippine women's bobsled team, consisting of pilot Rhea Joy Sumalpong from Tangub City, Misamis Occidental, and brakesman Shirley Salamagos from Itbayat, Batanes, is set to compete in the North ...

item.title: Ishaq Dar stresses united global response against growing Islamophobia on UN-designated day
item.description: On the International Day to Combat Islamophobia, observed globally on 15 March, Pakistan’s Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar raised grave concern over the sharp increase in Islamoph...

item.title: Celebrini Leads San Jose Sharks to 4-2 Victory Over Montreal Canadiens
item.description: Macklin Celebrini had an outstanding performance, scoring two goals and adding an assist to propel the San Jose Sharks to a 4-2 win over the Montreal Canadiens on Saturday night. Collin Graf contribut...

item.title: Recent Cybersecurity Breaches Include Accidental Access to FBI Epstein Files and Exposure of Private User Data
item.description: This week’s security news covers multiple significant cyber incidents and ongoing cybersecurity concerns. A foreign hacker unintentionally accessed the FBI’s Epstein files, which contained sensitive m...

item.title: Highlights from ABS-CBN's TV Patrol Regional Newscast on March 15, 2026
item.description: The March 15, 2026 edition of TV Patrol Regional from ABS-CBN brought together the day’s top regional stories from across the Philippines. As part of the country’s leading media network, the program c...

item.title: Sydney suburb Coogee becomes a hub for the Irish community
item.description: Coogee, a beachside suburb in Sydney, has earned the nickname 'County Coogee' due to its significant Irish population and cultural presence. Nearly 20% of its residents claim Irish ancestry, and the a...

item.title: A first-hand look inside Ponte City: From slum to community revival
item.description: Ponte City, Johannesburg’s iconic 54-storey skyscraper, has a story of extremes. Opened in 1975, it held the title of Africa’s tallest building for nearly five decades. Over time, neglect and social i...

item.title: Israel faces shortage of missile interceptors as Iranian attacks continue
item.description: Israel is reportedly facing a critical shortage of ballistic missile interceptors as it contends with ongoing Iranian attacks. US officials have confirmed that Israel entered the conflict with deplete...

item.title: Philippine Women’s Football Team Falls to Japan but Eyes World Cup Play-in
item.description: The Philippine women’s national football team, the Filipinas, faced a tough challenge against Japan in the quarterfinals of the 2026 AFC Women’s Asian Cup. Despite holding their own for over 40 minute...

item.title: Dennis Busulwa Ssebo and his role in making Singeli music national
item.description: Dennis Busulwa, commonly called Ssebo in Tanzania’s media circles, passed away on March 11, 2026. Known as a quiet yet powerful force behind the scenes, he served as Director of Operations and Busines...

item.title: Academic Challenges in Studying Unidentified Aerial Phenomena in the United States
item.description: Despite growing government attention on unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) in the United States, academic research on the topic faces significant barriers. In February 2026, former President Donal...

item.title: Dom Young scores second try for Newcastle Knights against Manly
item.description: In the latest NRL clash, Dom Young made a significant impact for the Newcastle Knights by scoring their second try against Manly. The match showcased Young's strength and agility as he powered over to...

item.title: Lib Dems call for a fully independent UK nuclear deterrent
item.description: The leader of the Liberal Democrats, Sir Ed Davey, has urged the UK government to establish a completely independent nuclear deterrent, aiming to end the country's reliance on the United States. Altho...
